description_long: This is a futuristic, compact airplane with a predominantly blue exterior accented by black and gray components. The fuselage is robust and angular, featuring a forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple dark-tinted windows. The wings are short and stubby, mounted mid-fuselage, and each wing houses a large, cylindrical engine nacelle with visible intake and exhaust details. The tail section is integrated into the main body, lacking a distinct vertical stabilizer, and the overall design suggests a focus on maneuverability and durability. The surface finish is smooth and slightly glossy, with visible panel lines and mechanical details that hint at reinforced construction. The airplane's design is reminiscent of a sci-fi or military urban aircraft, optimized for short-range operations in dense environments.
description: A compact, blue and black futuristic airplane with mid-mounted short wings and large cylindrical engines, featuring a forward-enclosed cockpit and integrated tail design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the angular nose, forward cockpit windows, and the prominent cylindrical engines extending from each side.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the mid-mounted wings, engine nacelle details, and the integrated tail section.
description_view_2: Top-down view revealing the overall planform, wing placement, and the symmetrical layout of engines and fuselage.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the engine and wing configuration, as well as the smooth, armored fuselage.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with short, straight wings and no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin cylindrical jet engines integrated into each wing root.
attribute_tail_design: Integrated tail with no distinct vertical stabilizer, resembling a blended or tailless configuration.
attribute_landing_gear_type: No visible landing gear present in the images.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple dark-tinted windows, likely single-seat.
